claim: The transfer layer should expose one internal compatibility API before its implementation files are moved.
mechanism: Transfer logic is one of the repo's highest-risk surfaces because it touches recursive improvement and cross-chip mutation. Wrapping its current entry points behind a single namespace lets callers depend on one stable seam while preserving the existing implementation. That reduces the chance that later structural work rewires recursive-improvement behavior accidentally while chasing imports.
boundary: This does not validate the transfer algorithms themselves. It only creates a cleaner internal boundary for future movement and review.
